title: Move OCI runtime implementation behind an interface
body:
For future work, we need multiple implementations of the OCI runtime, not just a Conmon-wrapped runtime matching the runc CLI.

As part of this, do some refactoring on the interface for exec (move to a struct, not a massive list of arguments). Also, add 'all' support to Kill and Stop (supported by runc and used a bit internally for removing containers).

Next step is to add a null runtime implementation that we can use for containers that want, say, runc - but it's not available on the system.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@haircommander @vrothberg @baude PTAL
This is a great start. I definitely think we need to think carefully about the interface that is being defined (Exec's struct comes to mind, as well as Conmon*()).

I'm gonna drop the WIP. This is big enough as is. The followon work (a null runtime to handle cases where a container wants a runtime but it isn't installed - e.g. F31 silverblue / runc) can come later.

Another bit of refactoring work for later: we should make all calls to the OCI runtime use a timeout. And we should consider lowering the 240 second Conmon timeout - if Conmon is stuck waiting to give us the container PID, I think a 60 second wait is sufficient, and the extra 180 doesn't really have a practical benefit.
Addressed feedback, squashed down commits
Tests should be going green now, I think
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #4216) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
Checkpoint failure is in `rm -fa`. We are consistently seeing the first container (`podman run --rm alpine top`) removing itself as `podman rm -fa` runs.
I suspect it's some sort of race between `podman rm` and the cleanup process doing `--rm` but I can't spot what is stopping the first container.

Got it. Both restored containers have the same cgroup path set in the spec. `podman rm` now uses `--all` so removing that first container with `runc` hits every process in the cgroup. Since both restored containers share a cgroup, this stops container 2, and the cleanup process removes it in parallel.
Latest commit should fix it.
LGTM if vrothberg and @haircommander are happy with your thinking on their comments, I'd say merge this in and then touch up stuff later.  Really nice work @mheon.
Finally green, let's get this landed
Can we get to the point where this code could be shared with CRI-O?
That's the end goal - @haircommander wants to unify the implementations to reduce code reuse. Would let Podman pick up the CRI-O Kata runtime implementation, too.
Then shouldn't these be moved to pkg/oci or something like that. I would want the KATA stuff moved down here, rather then vendor from CRI-O down.
@rhatdan @baude @giuseppe @vrothberg PTAL and merge
We can't move the OCI code right now - would be a massive rework to not use internal container structs. That's future work.

LGTM
But I want @haircommander to comment.
LGTM
/lgtm
